2484	  % iff + global-Presentation case), and (gap 2) the affine transport U ↦ Spec Γ(X,U) with
2485	  % Ab-valued ↔ ModuleCat-valued section reconciliation. Both need blueprint blocks +
2486	  % Lean decls before this keystone can be pinned. See task_results/.../QuotScheme.md.
2487	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes" (chapter tag 020J), \S 7
2488	  % "Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines", Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves},
2489	  % item (4) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691-L702).
2490	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2491	  % "Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$
2492	  %  be the sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules
2493	  %  associated to $M$.
2494	  %  [...]
2495	  %  \item For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
2496	  %  as an $R_f$-module."
2497	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", \S 7, Lemma on the sheaves
2498	  \(\widetilde M\) on \(\Spec(R)\), item (4).}
2499	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a \emph{quasi-coherent} sheaf
2500	  of modules on \(X\). Let \(U \subseteq X\) be an
2501	  affine open and \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then:
2502	  \begin{enumerate}
2503	    \item \(\Gamma\bigl(X, D(f)\bigr)\) is the localization \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)
2504	      of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\)
2505	      (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); and
2506	    \item the section-restriction map
2507	      \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}\bigl(D(f)\bigr)\) exhibits
2508	      \(\mathcal{M}(D(f))\) as the localized module
2509	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\mathcal{M}(U)\); that is, it is a localization
2510	      of the \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(\mathcal{M}(U)\) at the submonoid
2511	      \(\mathrm{powers}\,f = \{1, f, f^2, \dots\}\).
2512	  \end{enumerate}
2513	\end{lemma}
2514	
2515	\begin{proof}
2516	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2517	  Part (1) is \cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, the Mathlib statement
2518	  that on an affine open \(U\) the sections over the basic open \(D(f)\) form the
2519	  localization of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\).
2520	
2521	  For part (2), identify the affine open \(U\) with \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\). Over
2522	  this affine, a quasi-coherent sheaf is \(\widetilde{N}\) for the
2523	  \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\), the sheaf associated to
2524	  \(N\) (no finiteness of \(N\) is required for this step). The Spec-local
2525	  computation of the sections of \(\widetilde{N}\) on a
2526	  basic open gives \(\Gamma\bigl(D(f), \widetilde{N}\bigr) = N_f\) as an
2527	  \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)-module, and the restriction map
2528	  \(\Gamma(\Spec \Gamma(X,U), \widetilde{N}) = N \to N_f\) is precisely the
2529	  localization homomorphism \(N \to (\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1} N\). Transporting
2530	  this identification across the affine isomorphism
2531	  \(U \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) -- under which \(\mathcal{M}|_U\) corresponds to
2532	  \(\widetilde{N}\) by quasi-coherence and the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq U\)
2533	  corresponds to the standard open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec\Gamma(X,U)\) -- shows
2534	  that the restriction \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}(D(f))\) is the
2535	  localization of \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\), i.e.\ it is a
2536	  localized module at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\). The substantive content is this
2537	  transport of the Spec-local localization fact for the sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\)
2538	  to a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}\) via the affine identification.
2539	\end{proof}

2549	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert

2551	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L468-L471).
2552	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2553	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
2554	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
2555	  %  be any coherent sheaf on

2557	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
2558	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\). The
2559	  \emph{schematic support} of \(\mathcal{F}\) is the closed subscheme of \(X\)
2560	  cut out by its annihilator ideal sheaf (\cref{def:modules_annihilator}):
2561	  \[

2563	    \;=\; V\bigl(\mathrm{Ann}(\mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\hookrightarrow\; X,
2564	  \]
2565	  equipped with its canonical closed immersion into \(X\). This realises
2566	  Nitsure's ``schematic support of \(\mathcal{F}\)'' as a closed subscheme of
2567	  \(X\).
2568	\end{definition}

155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
160	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
161	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
162	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
163	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
164	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
165	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
166	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
168	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
169	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
170	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
171	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
172	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
173	  %  the function
174	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
175	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
176	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
177	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
179	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
180	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
181	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
182	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
183	  \[
184	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
185	  \]
186	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
187	  the tensor product of sections
188	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
189	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
190	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
191	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
192	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
193	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
194	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
195	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
196	  polynomial ring).
197	\end{definition}
198

200	[Section graded module]
201	  \label{def:sectionGradedModule}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule}
203	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing}
204	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
205	  % Polynomials" (read from
206	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
207	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
208	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
209	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
210	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
211	  %  the function
212	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
213	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
214	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
215	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
216	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
217	  I.7.}
218	  Let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on the fibre \(X_s\) and \(L_s\) a
219	  line bundle on \(X_s\). The \emph{section graded module} of \(\mathcal{F}_s\)
220	  twisted by \(L_s\) is the graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module
221	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing})
222	  \[
223	    M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)
224	    \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
225	  \]
226	  with degree-\(m\) component the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space
227	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) and scalar action given
228	  by the section tensor product
229	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
230	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m'})
231	  \to \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\). Its graded Hilbert
232	  function is \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)}
233	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
234	\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}[Basic-open restriction of a $\widetilde{N}$ sheaf is a module localization]
  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes" (tag 020J), \S 7, Lemma on the sheaves
  % \widetilde M on Spec(R), item (4) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691-L702).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$ as an $R_f$-module."
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", \S 7, item (4); Spec-local core of
  \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}, part (2).}
  Let \(N\) be an \(R\)-module and \(f \in R\). Then the presheaf restriction map of the
  associated sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\) on \(\Spec R\) from the global sections
  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, \top)\) to the sections on the basic open \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\)
  exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde{N}, D(f))\) as the localized module
  \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1} N\); that is, it is \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\)
  over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  Mathlib supplies the localization-out-of-\(N\) instance
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for the map
  \((\mathrm{tilde.toOpen}\,N\,(D f))\) (the canonical map \(N \to \Gamma(\widetilde N, D(f))\)).
  Since \(\mathrm{tilde.toOpen}\,N\,\top\) is an isomorphism and
  \(\mathrm{tilde.toOpen}\,N\,(D f) = \mathrm{tilde.toOpen}\,N\,\top \,\circ\, \mathrm{restriction}\),
  precomposing the localization map with the inverse isomorphism transfers the
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) property to the restriction map (Mathlib's
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.of\_linearEquiv\_right}\)).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Basic-open restriction localizes for a sheaf in the essential image of $\widetilde{(-)}$]
  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \uses{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
  \textit{Affine engine of \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}: the basic-open
  restriction localizes for any module sheaf that is \(\widetilde{N}\) up to isomorphism.}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) such that the counit
  \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism (equivalently, \(M\) lies in the essential image of
  \(\widetilde{(-)}\); Mathlib's \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_iff}\)), and let \(f \in R\). Then the
  presheaf restriction of \(M\) from \(\Gamma(M, \top)\) to \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) is
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  The image of \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) under \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}\) is an isomorphism;
  forgetting to the presheaf gives a natural isomorphism \(\psi\) whose components are isomorphisms
  of \(R\)-modules. Its naturality square for the inclusion \(D(f) \hookrightarrow \top\)
  intertwines the restriction maps of \(\widetilde{N}\) (for \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\)) and of \(M\).
  Transporting \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict} across the two component isomorphisms
  (post-compose by \(\mathrm{of\_linearEquiv}\), pre-compose by \(\mathrm{of\_linearEquiv\_right}\))
  identifies the restriction of \(M\) with a localization map, hence it is
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent on an affine has trivial $\widetilde{(-)}$-counit]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent}
  % NOTE (iter-026): this is the keystone's missing descent ingredient (gap 1). The Lean decl
  % does NOT yet exist; it is the explicit Mathlib-gradient target for the QUOT mathlib-build lane.
  % Mathlib supplies `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` (counit iso ↔ essential image) and
  % `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (M carrying a GLOBAL Presentation), and the Tilde.lean
  % source comment states the QCoh ≃ Mod identification "will later be shown" — i.e. it is absent
  % at the pinned commit. The substance below is: quasi-coherence (LOCAL cokernel-of-frees data)
  % ⟹ a GLOBAL Presentation on the affine Spec R, the hard direction of QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R.
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes" / "Schemes" — quasi-coherent sheaves on an
  % affine scheme are associated to their global sections (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex).
  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  By Mathlib's \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\) it suffices to produce a global
  presentation of \(M\): a pair of free module sheaves and an exact cokernel diagram
  \(\mathcal{O}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}^{(I)} \to M \to 0\). Quasi-coherence provides, on a cover of
  \(\Spec R\) by basic opens, local presentations of \(M\) as cokernels of maps of free sheaves.
  Because \(\Spec R\) is affine -- in particular quasi-compact and quasi-separated -- these local
  cokernel-of-frees data assemble to a global presentation: pass to a finite subcover, take global
  sections to obtain a generating family and a relation module over \(R\), and rebuild the
  presentation globally as the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-image of an \(R\)-module free presentation. Apply
  \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\) to conclude. This globalization step is the
  hard direction of the affine equivalence \(\mathrm{QCoh}(\Spec R) \simeq \mathrm{Mod}(R)\) and is
  the Mathlib-absent content; the mathlib-build lane develops it bottom-up.
\end{proof}

# mathlib-analogist directive — iter-026 (Quot-Foundations)

## Mode: api-alignment

## Question
We need to build, axiom-clean in the project, the lemma (gap1):

  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent` :
    for `M : (Spec R).Modules` with `M.IsQuasicoherent`, `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.

Equivalently (by Mathlib `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff`): a quasi-coherent module sheaf on `Spec R` lies in the
essential image of `tilde : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules`. This is the hard direction of the affine
equivalence QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R.

What Mathlib provides at the pinned commit (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean`):
- `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage M`.
- `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation (M) (P : M.Presentation) : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — needs a GLOBAL
  `SheafOfModules.Presentation` (a cokernel-of-free-sheaves diagram).
- `presentationTilde` / `instance : (tilde M).IsQuasicoherent` — the EASY direction.
- The source comment says the QCoh = essential-image identification "will later be shown" (i.e. absent).

`IsQuasicoherent` (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` / `QuasicoherentData`) gives only LOCAL
cokernel-of-frees presentations on an open cover.

## What I need from you (api-alignment)
1. **Does Mathlib already have a route from `IsQuasicoherent` (or `QuasicoherentData`) to a global
   `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on an affine / quasi-compact-quasi-separated scheme?** Search for:
   `Presentation`, `IsQuasicoherent`, `QuasicoherentData`, `essImage`, global-generation /
   `Module.Finite`-free-presentation lemmas, and any `AffineScheme`/`Spec`-specific quasi-coherent
   descent. Name exact declarations (full names + file) if they exist.
2. If no single lemma exists, **what is the canonical Mathlib idiom/decomposition** to assemble a global
   presentation from local cokernel-of-frees data on `Spec R` (qcqs)? E.g. is there a "globally generated
   on affine", "sections generate", `Γ`-adjunction counit-epi, or a `quasiSeparated`/`quasiCompact`
   gluing-of-presentations lemma? Give the concrete chain of Mathlib declarations a prover would compose.
3. **Is there a SHORTER route** that avoids constructing a `Presentation` at all — e.g. proving
   `(tilde.functor R).essImage M` directly from `IsQuasicoherent M` via the unit/counit of
   `tilde.adjunction`, or via `M.fromTildeΓ` being a local iso (iso on each basic-open stalk/section) and
   then iso globally? Name the Mathlib lemmas (local-iso ⟹ iso for sheaf morphisms, `isIso_of_isIso_app`,
   stalk-wise iso, etc.).
4. Verdict: is gap1 a **single-lemma** build, a **short chain** (name it), or **genuine multi-step
   descent** with no current Mathlib idiom (in which case give the cleanest decomposition into named
   sub-lemmas a mathlib-build lane should target)?

The output seeds the gap1 blueprint sketch (`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ` in
`Picard_QuotScheme.tex`), which the blueprint-reviewer flagged as hand-waving the globalization. Write a
persistent `analogies/<slug>.md` so the next iter's planner has the concrete route.

**Verdict:** gap1 is **genuine multi-step descent**, not a single lemma or short chain. All three candidate routes — global `Presentation` (Route A, strictly *harder*, avoid), stalk-iso (Route B), and section-localization (Route C, project-aligned, preferred) — funnel through **one Mathlib-absent fact** (Stacks 01HA): for QC `M` on `Spec R`, `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Confirmed `fromTildeΓ` appears *only* in `Tilde.lean` and its source comment admits the QCoh=essImage identification is unbuilt.

**Decomposition for the build lane:**

91	## The irreducible content (the actual gap)
92	
93	All three routes funnel through ONE Mathlib-absent fact (Stacks tag 01HA / Hartshorne II.5.2):
94	
95	> **For a quasi-coherent `M` on `Spec R` and `f : R`, the restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))`
96	> is `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)`** (equivalently `M.stalk p` is the localization
97	> of `Γ(M,⊤)` at `p.primeCompl`).
98	
99	Proof skeleton from `QuasicoherentData` (genuine multi-step descent):
100	1. **Refine the QC cover to basic opens.** `PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`; restrict each
101	   `(M.over (X i)).Presentation` to a basic open `D(g_a) ⊆ X_i` via `Presentation.map` along the
102	   `pushforward`/`over` restriction functor (Quasicoherent.lean:179). `CompactSpace (PrimeSpectrum R)`
103	   (Spectrum/Prime/Topology.lean:291) ⟹ a **finite** subcover; the `g_a` generate the unit ideal.
104	2. **Locally `M` is a tilde sheaf.** On `D(g_a) = Spec R_{g_a}`, the local presentation +
105	   `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` ⟹ `M.over (D g_a) ≅ tilde(N_a)`, `N_a = Γ(M, D(g_a))`,
106	   over the ring `Γ(Spec R, D(g_a)) = R_{g_a}` (`AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalization_basicOpen`,
107	   AffineScheme.lean:650).
108	3. **Sheaf equalizer + flat descent.** Sheaf condition over the finite cover {D(g_a)} gives
109	   `0 → Γ(M,⊤) → ∏ N_a → ∏ Γ(M, D(g_a g_b))`; localizing at `powers f` is exact
110	   (`IsLocalization.flat`, Flat/Localization.lean:36; localization commutes with finite limits)
111	   and matches the same equalizer for `Γ(M,D(f))`, yielding `Γ(M,⊤)_f ≅ Γ(M,D(f))`.
112	4. Stalk version: `M.stalk p = colim_{D(g)∋p} Γ(M,D(g)) = colim N_g = N_p`.

2488	  % "Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines", Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves},
2489	  % item (4) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L691-L702).
2490	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2491	  % "Let $R$ be a ring. Let $M$ be an $R$-module. Let $\widetilde M$
2492	  %  be the sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$-modules
2493	  %  associated to $M$.
2494	  %  [...]

2497	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", \S 7, Lemma on the sheaves
2498	  \(\widetilde M\) on \(\Spec(R)\), item (4).}
2499	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a \emph{quasi-coherent} sheaf
2500	  of modules on \(X\). Let \(U \subseteq X\) be an
2501	  affine open and \(f \in \Gamma(X, U)\). Then:
2502	  \begin{enumerate}
2503	    \item \(\Gamma\bigl(X, D(f)\bigr)\) is the localization \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)
2504	      of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\)
2505	      (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}); and
2506	    \item the section-restriction map
2507	      \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}\bigl(D(f)\bigr)\) exhibits
2508	      \(\mathcal{M}(D(f))\) as the localized module
2509	      \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\mathcal{M}(U)\); that is, it is a localization
2510	      of the \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(\mathcal{M}(U)\) at the submonoid
2511	      \(\mathrm{powers}\,f = \{1, f, f^2, \dots\}\).
2512	  \end{enumerate}
2513	\end{lemma}
2514	
2515	\begin{proof}
2516	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
2517	  Part (1) is \cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}, the Mathlib statement
2518	  that on an affine open \(U\) the sections over the basic open \(D(f)\) form the
2519	  localization of \(\Gamma(X,U)\) away from \(f\).
2520	
2521	  For part (2), identify the affine open \(U\) with \(\Spec \Gamma(X, U)\). Over
2522	  this affine, a quasi-coherent sheaf is \(\widetilde{N}\) for the
2523	  \(\Gamma(X,U)\)-module \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\), the sheaf associated to
2524	  \(N\) (no finiteness of \(N\) is required for this step). The Spec-local
2525	  computation of the sections of \(\widetilde{N}\) on a
2526	  basic open gives \(\Gamma\bigl(D(f), \widetilde{N}\bigr) = N_f\) as an
2527	  \((\Gamma(X,U))_f\)-module, and the restriction map
2528	  \(\Gamma(\Spec \Gamma(X,U), \widetilde{N}) = N \to N_f\) is precisely the
2529	  localization homomorphism \(N \to (\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1} N\). Transporting
2530	  this identification across the affine isomorphism
2531	  \(U \cong \Spec \Gamma(X, U)\) -- under which \(\mathcal{M}|_U\) corresponds to
2532	  \(\widetilde{N}\) by quasi-coherence and the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq U\)
2533	  corresponds to the standard open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec\Gamma(X,U)\) -- shows
2534	  that the restriction \(\mathcal{M}(U) \to \mathcal{M}(D(f))\) is the
2535	  localization of \(N = \mathcal{M}(U)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\), i.e.\ it is a
2536	  localized module at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\). The substantive content is this
2537	  transport of the Spec-local localization fact for the sheaf \(\widetilde{N}\)
2538	  to a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{M}\) via the affine identification.
2539	\end{proof}

2607	  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
2608	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
2609	  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;

2613	\begin{proof}
2614	  By Mathlib's \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\) it suffices to produce a global
2615	  presentation of \(M\): a pair of free module sheaves and an exact cokernel diagram

\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent on an affine has trivial $\widetilde{(-)}$-counit]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent}
  % NOTE: this is the keystone's missing descent ingredient (gap 1). The Lean decl
  % does NOT yet exist. Mathlib supplies `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` (counit iso ↔ essential image) and
  % `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (M carrying a GLOBAL Presentation), and the Tilde.lean
  % source comment states the QCoh ≃ Mod identification "will later be shown" — i.e. it is absent
  % at the pinned commit. The substance below is: quasi-coherence (LOCAL cokernel-of-frees data)
  % ⟹ a GLOBAL Presentation on the affine Spec R, the hard direction of QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R.
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine},
  % \S\,Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1279--1284).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
  % be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
  % $\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the $R$-module $\Gamma(X,
  % \mathcal{F})$."
  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  By Mathlib's \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\) it suffices to produce a global
  presentation of \(M\): a pair of free module sheaves and an exact cokernel diagram
  \(\mathcal{O}^{(J)} \to \mathcal{O}^{(I)} \to M \to 0\). Quasi-coherence provides, on a cover of
  \(\Spec R\) by basic opens, local presentations of \(M\) as cokernels of maps of free sheaves.
  Because \(\Spec R\) is affine -- in particular quasi-compact and quasi-separated -- these local
  cokernel-of-frees data assemble to a global presentation: pass to a finite subcover, take global
  sections to obtain a generating family and a relation module over \(R\), and rebuild the
  presentation globally as the \(\widetilde{(-)}\)-image of an \(R\)-module free presentation. Apply
  \(\mathrm{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\) to conclude. This globalization step is the
  hard direction of the affine equivalence \(\mathrm{QCoh}(\Spec R) \simeq \mathrm{Mod}(R)\) and is
  the Mathlib-absent content.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Section-localization for a quasi-coherent sheaf on $\Spec R$ (G1-core)]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent}
  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
  % NOTE: the genuine Mathlib-absent foundational gap; the Lean decl does NOT yet exist. This is the
  % affine instance (X = Spec R, U = ⊤) of \cref{lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen}, proved
  % DIRECTLY from QuasicoherentData (no IsIso/Presentation assumption), and is what gap1 and the
  % general keystone both reduce to. Build target for the QUOT mathlib-build lane (route in
  % analogies/quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md, Route C / Stacks 01HA).
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", tag 01HA (\S Quasi-coherent sheaves,
  % "$\mathcal{F}(D(f)) = \mathcal{F}(X)_f$ for $\mathcal{F}$ quasi-coherent on affine $X$")
  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex / stacks-properties.tex). Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.2.
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01HA; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.2.}
  Let \(M\) be a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\). Then the section
  restriction map \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) exhibits \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) as the
  localized module \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\); i.e.\ it is
  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
\end{lemma}
% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: the descent is the standard cover-by-basic-opens + flat-localization argument;
% transcribed in project notation below (Stacks 01HA proof; references/stacks-properties.tex).
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
  Write \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\). The map is built and verified in four steps (the cover-refine →
  local-tilde → flat-equalizer descent):
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \emph{Refine the quasi-coherence cover to basic opens.} The quasi-coherence datum gives an
      open cover of \(\Spec R\) on each member of which \(M\) is a cokernel of free module sheaves.
      Basic opens \(D(g)\) form a basis of \(\Spec R\), so refine to a cover by basic opens; since
      \(\Spec R\) is quasi-compact, finitely many \(D(g_1), \dots, D(g_n)\) suffice, and the \(g_a\)
      generate the unit ideal.
    \item \emph{Locally \(M\) is a $\widetilde{(-)}$ sheaf.} On each \(D(g_a) \cong \Spec R_{g_a}\)
      the local cokernel-of-frees presentation makes \(M|_{D(g_a)} \cong \widetilde{N_a}\) for the
      \(R_{g_a}\)-module \(N_a = \Gamma(M, D(g_a))\), using that \(\Gamma(\Spec R, D(g_a)) = R_{g_a}\)
      (\cref{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}).
    \item \emph{Sheaf equalizer + flat descent.} The sheaf condition over the finite cover
      \(\{D(g_a)\}\) presents \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\) as the equalizer
      \(N \to \prod_a N_a \rightrightarrows \prod_{a,b} \Gamma(M, D(g_a g_b))\). Localizing this
      finite equalizer at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) is exact (localization is flat, hence preserves
      finite limits), and the localized diagram is the same equalizer computing \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\)
      over the cover \(\{D(g_a) \cap D(f)\}\). Hence \(N_f \cong \Gamma(M, D(f))\), compatibly with
      the restriction map.
    \item Therefore \(\Gamma(M,\top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is the localization \(N \to N_f\), i.e.\
      \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\).
  \end{enumerate}
  The flat-localization-of-a-finite-equalizer step is the substantive content; everything else is
  the affine dictionary already available.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Quasi-coherent on an affine has trivial $\widetilde{(-)}$-counit (gap1)]
  \label{lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent}
  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
  % NOTE: the Lean decl does NOT yet exist. gap1 now follows from G1-core
  % (\cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization}) by the cheap stalk/section assembly below — all
  % remaining ingredients are Mathlib glue. Mathlib supplies `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff`,
  % `isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`, `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`,
  % and `FullyFaithful.reflectsIsomorphisms` (via the project's SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful).
  % Route: analogies/quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md, "G1-assemble".
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Schemes", Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1279--1284).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
  % be an affine scheme. Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module. Then
  % $\mathcal{F}$ is isomorphic to the sheaf associated to the $R$-module $\Gamma(X,
  % \mathcal{F})$."
  \textit{The affine equivalence QCoh\((\Spec R)\) \(\simeq\) Mod\((R)\): a quasi-coherent module
  sheaf on \(\Spec R\) is \(\widetilde{\Gamma(M)}\) up to canonical isomorphism.}
  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) that is quasi-coherent
  (\(\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,M\)). Then the counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism;
  equivalently \(M\) lies in the essential image of \(\widetilde{(-)} : \mathrm{Mod}(R) \to (\Spec
  R).\mathrm{Modules}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization, lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}
  Set \(N = \Gamma(M, \top)\). The counit \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ} : \widetilde N \to M\) is an
  isomorphism iff its image under the fully faithful \(\mathrm{modulesSpecToSheaf}\) is, and the
  latter is a morphism of sheaves of \(R\)-modules on \(\Spec R\), so it suffices to check it is an
  isomorphism on each basic open \(D(f)\) (basic opens form a basis; a sheaf morphism that is an
  isomorphism on a basis of opens is an isomorphism). On \(D(f)\) the component is
  \(N_f \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\): the source \(\Gamma(\widetilde N, D(f)) = N_f\) localizes \(N\) at
  \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) by \cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict}, and the target
  \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\) localizes \(N\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) by
  \cref{lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization} (G1-core); the map between them is the canonical one
  (compatibility \(\mathrm{toOpen\_fromTildeΓ\_app}\)). Two localizations of \(N\) at the same
  submonoid intertwined by the localization maps are uniquely isomorphic
  (\(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv}\)), so the component is an isomorphism. Hence
  \(M.\mathrm{fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

# Iter 026 — Plan (Quot-Foundations)

## TL;DR

**First prover iter in the freshly-extracted project** (iter-025 was DAG-wiring; the FBC/QUOT task_results
were the parent's iter-024). I reconciled the live state, then drove the iter's load-bearing call: the QUOT
keystone's blocker `gap1` was hand-wavy, so a **mathlib-analogist** API study collapsed it to a concrete
foundation **G1-core** (section-localization for QC sheaves on `Spec R`, Stacks 01HA) — which is ALSO the
keystone's affine instance, and it exposed a **blueprint circularity I had introduced** (keystone `\uses`
gap1, backwards). I rewrote the QUOT chapter to the honest order **G1-core → gap1 → keystone**, cleared
coverage debt (the 2 iter-024 affine engines), fixed the FBC `inner_value_eq` must-fix (pre-subst route),
and — via the sanctioned same-iter fast-path re-review — cleared the HARD GATE for all three lanes.
**Dispatched 3 parallel import-independent lanes:** FBC `inner_value_eq` (pre-subst), QUOT **G1-core**
(mathlib-build), GR-glue `Grassmannian.scheme` (mathlib-build).

## State at entry (verified live)

- FBC: 3 `inner_eCancel` atoms + Seam B closed axiom-clean (parent iter-024). Live sorries: dead
  `fstar_reindex_legs` @1421, `inner_value_eq` @1627 (literal-form-lock), `gstar_transpose` @1810 (gated),
  affine @1983, FBC-B @2005.
- QUOT: 2 affine engines built axiom-clean; keystone not built (gap1 bottleneck). 4 protected stubs.
- GF: `genericFlatness` @2173 sole sorry, gated on keystone. `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` clean.
- GR/GHS: 0 sorries (charts+transitions+cocycle DONE; glue not started).
- All modules GREEN; one commit (extraction).

## Subagents this iter (5; all returned)

- **progress-critic `iter026`** — FBC **CHURNING** (PARTIAL×4, net sorry +1 over K=4); corrective =
  blueprint expansion (applied BEFORE the verdict as the pre-subst route); *"the dispatch IS the
  corrective"*; escalation = mathlib-analogist if still stuck next iter. QUOT-keystone + GR-glue UNCLEAR.
  Dispatch sanity OK.
- **blueprint-clean `iter026`** — scrubbed iteration/memory leakage from the FBC ordering paragraph + the
  3 new QUOT blocks; validated the Stacks §7 item-(4) quote, added a `% SOURCE QUOTE` for gap1 from
  `stacks-schemes.tex` lemma-quasi-coherent-affine.
- **blueprint-reviewer `iter026`** (whole) — FBC PASS, GR PASS-for-dispatch, QUOT **CONDITIONAL** (gap1
  globalization hand-waved).
- **mathlib-analogist `gap1`** (api-alignment, the QUOT corrective) — gap1 = genuine multi-step descent,
  no Mathlib idiom; all routes funnel through G1-core (Stacks 01HA); gave the concrete 4-step route and
  flagged the circularity. Persistent `analogies/quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md`.
- **blueprint-reviewer `quot-fastpath`** (scoped, same-iter fast path) — after the rewrite: **HARD GATE
  CLEARS** for a G1-core mathlib-build (0 must-fix; soon-finding = add `\mathlibok` anchors, non-blocking).

## Decision made

### QUOT lane retarget: build G1-core, not the hand-wavy gap1/general keystone
- **Option chosen:** dispatch the QUOT mathlib-build lane at **G1-core**
  (`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — the section-localization for QC sheaves on `Spec R`
  — rather than the general keystone or the gap1 `IsIso fromTildeΓ` directly.
- **Why:** the mathlib-analogist established (with a Mathlib `grep` + API map) that gap1 and the general
  keystone BOTH reduce to G1-core, which is the single irreducible Mathlib-absent fact, and that the
  global-`Presentation` route (Route A) is strictly *harder*. G1-core has a concrete 4-step route; gap1 +
  keystone are then cheap glue on top. Building G1-core "pays twice" (GF-G1 ∩ QUOT-annihilator).
- **Trade-off:** G1-core is genuine descent (flat-localization of a finite sheaf-equalizer); mathlib-build
  may hand off a finer decomposition rather than fully close — acceptable per the Mathlib-gradient policy
  (no typed sorry; a precise decomposition is real progress).
- **Reversal signal:** if the prover reports the flat-equalizer step needs Mathlib infra that is itself
  absent (e.g. no usable finite-sheaf-condition-as-equalizer for `SheafOfModules`), pivot to the stalk
  route (Route B) or escalate that sub-gap as the next mathlib-build target.

### FBC CHURNING — corrective honored, NOT a reworded re-dispatch
The progress-critic's CHURNING corrective is **blueprint expansion**, which I applied as the pre-subst
"Order of operations" paragraph (the exact iter-024 checker must-fix), cleared by both reviewers. The
critic explicitly endorses dispatching on it this iter ("the dispatch IS the corrective"). This is the
sanctioned execute-the-corrective response, not another helper round. **Tripwire:** if `inner_value_eq`
is still stuck next iter, the next move is a mathlib-analogist cross-domain consult on the literal-form
lock — recorded so the next planner does not re-dispatch FBC blindly.

### Three lanes (parallelism directive honored)
FBC / QUOT / GR are different files, import-independent. Every QUOT-internal alternative (sectionGradedRing,
annihilator, P2, SNAP) is Mathlib-blocked / gated / protected — verified this iter — so G1-core is QUOT's
only gate-clear forward step; GR-glue is the one fully-unblocked clean lane. This is the honest maximum of
gate-clear parallel work, not a padded count.

## Subagent skips

- strategy-critic: routes are unchanged in substance — my STRATEGY edits are within-route status
  refinements (GR-glue NEXT→ACTIVE; naming gap1/G1-core and the SheafOfModules-tensor-powers gap as
  explicit Mathlib-gradient targets), no route swap / new route / decomposition change. The prior
  (iter-024) verdict was SOUND on all active routes; the one live CHALLENGE (QUOT Serre/S1 canonicity) is
  gated and NOT dispatched this iter (no S1 prover). Re-running would restate the same gated caveat with
  no new signal. The load-bearing strategic question this iter (gap1 tractability) was answered by the
  mathlib-analogist, the correct tool for it.

## Tool substitutions

- `archon-informal-agent.py` is unavailable (no DEEPSEEK/MOONSHOT/OPENROUTER/OPENAI/GEMINI key in env).
  For the gap1 route question I used the **mathlib-analogist** subagent (api-alignment) instead — the
  catalog equivalent for a Mathlib-API/route study — which is strictly better here (it greps live Mathlib
  rather than recalling from training). Recorded per the anti-fabrication substitution rule.

## Notes for iter-027
- If G1-core lands: chain gap1 + general keystone (gap2 transport), add the `\mathlibok` anchors, then
  open GF-G1 (shared-extraction decision first).
- If `gstar_transpose` closes: FBC dead-code refactor, then affine @1983 → FBC-B @2005.
- If `Grassmannian.scheme` lands: `lem:gr_separated` + `lem:gr_proper`.
- SheafOfModules tensor powers remains the SNAP blocker (Mathlib-gradient sub-build, owed).

# Iter 026 — Objectives (per-lane detail)

## Lane 1 — FBC `inner_value_eq` (FlatBaseChange.lean) [fine-grained]

Target: de-sorry `base_change_mate_inner_value_eq` @1627, then `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` @1810.
Blueprint: `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` (`lem:base_change_mate_inner_value_eq`,
`lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel` — new "Order of operations" paragraph).

Recipe (pre-subst route): apply `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp e.hom (Spec.map inclA)` to distribute the
`(g')`-unit WHILE `g'` is the free composite `e.hom ≫ Spec inclA` — before the codomain read substitutes
the literal `pullback.fst/snd` legs and locks the unit into `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ Spec includeLeft`.
Cancel with the 3 already-closed atoms on the free-form factors; then read through `codomain_read`; then
Seam 1 (`base_change_mate_unit_value`) + the `inclA∘φ=inclR'∘ψ` transport lands on ρ. Fallback: positional
`conv`/congruence on the locked factor.

Prior dead ends (do not repeat): whole-goal `rfl`/`simp`; per-generator brute force; `rw`/`simp only` /
`have…rw` matching the atom LHS against the post-codomain-read locked goal (literal-form lock, memory
`fbc-subst-legs-literal-form-lock`). Do NOT route through `fstar_reindex`/`_legs` (dead @1421).

Success bar: `inner_value_eq` closed via pre-subst, ideally `gstar_transpose`. Partial OK (leave the
distributed-but-uncancelled state). Out of scope: affine @1983, FBC-B @2005.

## Lane 2 — QUOT G1-core (QuotScheme.lean) [mathlib-build]

Target: build `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent` (new decl).
Blueprint: `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`). Full route:
`analogies/quot-qcoh-affine-globalization.md` (G1-core + G1-assemble).

Statement: QC `M : (Spec R).Modules`, `f : R` ⟹ `Γ(M,⊤)→Γ(M,D(f))` is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` /R.
4 steps: (1) refine QuasicoherentData cover to basic opens (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`) + finite
subcover (`CompactSpace (PrimeSpectrum R)`); (2) local-tilde on each `D(g_a)` via
`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` + `isLocalization_basicOpen`; (3) flat localization (`IsLocalization.flat`)
of the finite sheaf-equalizer `Γ(M,⊤)→∏N_a⇉∏Γ(M,D(g_a g_b))` matches the `Γ(M,D(f))` equalizer ⟹
`Γ(M,⊤)_f ≅ Γ(M,D(f))`; (4) conclude. No sorry; if blocked, hand off the precise sub-gap (likely the
finite-sheaf-equalizer packaging for `SheafOfModules`). Optionally chain gap1
(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`) via the G1-assemble glue. Out of scope: 4 protected stubs, SNAP,
annihilator, Grassmannian nodes.

## Lane 3 — GR-glue (GrassmannianCells.lean) [mathlib-build]

Target: build `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme` (new decl). Blueprint:
`Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`).

Populate a `Scheme.GlueData` (Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry/Gluing.lean`, extends `CategoryTheory.GlueData
Scheme`) from the DONE infra — index = `d`-subsets, objects = `affineChart`, overlaps `U^I_J`, transitions
= `transitionMap` `θ_{I,J}`, cocycle = `cocycleCondition` (`lem:gr_cocycle`) — then `Grassmannian.scheme :=
(…).glued`. No sorry; if a `GlueData` field obligation (open-immersion / pullback-compat / cocycle `t'`)
needs a bridge from the chart data, build it named axiom-clean. `lem:gr_separated`/`lem:gr_proper` are
next-iter follow-ons (not in scope unless budget remains after the glued scheme lands).
